principles: Length contributes to paddle efficiency, glide, rail line and turning arc.
Width contributes to planing area and stability, but placement of width matters.
Thickness contributes to foam, but foam distribution matters more than the centre number alone.
Volume is useful for comparison but cannot describe outline, rails, rocker, bottom contours or foam distribution.
Two boards with identical litres may feel radically different.
Beginner selection should not be reduced to a litres-per-kilogram formula.

less_length: greater reliance on positioning and paddling

notes: tail width influences lift, speed and control

thickness_and_rails.warning: Centre thickness alone does not reveal rail volume.
